title: The Attic
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:The Attic}} {{Class Custom Image|bg-image=Attic1.jpg|class=1|safety=Safe|entity=Devoid of Entities|hexagon=1}} '''The Attic''' is an anomalous location within the Backrooms. {{Blurimage|file=Attic1.jpg|caption=The first image ever taken of '''The Attic'''.}} {{Blurbackground|file=Attic1.jpg}} ==Description== '''The Attic''' takes on the appearance of a vast attic of inconsistent appearance that stretches out in arbitrary and constantly changing directions. The location itself is unusually distorted, with walls thicker than they should be, windows on the floor, lights hanging lower than they should, and its non-Euclidean geography constantly shifting, making navigation ever more troublesome. The air within '''The Attic''' has been reported to be very humid and warm, with temperatures staying at about 25°C (77°F). Windows within the level present a great variety of environments; however, most commonly, it is either a bright white void or a dark forest; these areas are currently inaccessible. '''The Attic''' is devoid of any life forms and, as a matter of fact, is devoid of any resources or other valuable objects outside. Furniture, including chairs and ottomans, as well as antique paintings reminiscent of Renaissance paintings, can be found within it. Additionally, outlets and functional light switches are also located within '''The Attic'''. Doors are uncommonly found within the level, which have random numbers burnt into them; the meaning of these numbers is unknown, as they do not lead to any other levels or locations. Wanderers should be wary of doors, as some of them are hooked to IEDs or have pits full of punji sticks beyond them. There is no indicator to determine whether a door is booby-trapped or not, and as such, it is advisable to avoid doors in general. Some of the paintings within the level also conceal hidden chambers, which are constructed from bricks and insulation foam. It is unknown if these booby traps and hidden chambers are artificial or are a part of '''The Attic''''s nature. {{Blurimage|file=Attic2.jpg|caption=An image taken of '''The Attic'''.}} '''The Attic''' has some unique rooms, such as the aforementioned hidden chambers; however, large corridors, with one wall entirely made of glass, large halls, abandoned bars, and bedrooms have also been discovered, although finding such areas is rare. Ventilation systems have been identified, though vent openings are few and far between. Entering, exploring, and exiting '''The Attic''' has proven to be inconsistent and generally unenjoyable due to a lack of incentive for exploring and settling the level. In fact, those who have wandered it have referred to it as "less of an area worth exploiting and studying, and more of an uncommon yet inconvenient obstacle through one's journey." All systems, such as GPS, also fail while in '''The Attic'''; moreover, the level has an isolating effect, where wanderers will not be able to meet others, similar to [[Level 0]], making any potential establishment of a colony impossible. ==Additional Information== Below are three accounts of certain wanderers exploring '''The Attic'''. Their whereabouts are currently unknown.
  • Log 1
  • Log 2
  • Log 3
The Writing on the Wall:
A documentation of a text found on the walls in '''The Attic'''. ---- This is... the strangest place I've ever seen. It's so mundane and yet so... strange. It's even more off than it should be. Some of the walls are too thick, some of the windows are skewed, the lights hang too low... so strangely mystifying.

I shouldn't have gone through that trapdoor in the [[Level 11|Endless City]]. I can only think about how worried sick my wife and daughter must be, and I can only hope I may return someday to them. For now, I am still trapped in this endless part of a terrifying edifice, left to wonder what I did wrong to be separated from my loved ones like this, again... ---- Wanderer's whereabouts are currently unknown.
Property #2546 of the Backroom Colonists
An audio log recovered from [[Level 39]] entailing the wanderer's discovery of '''The Attic'''. ---- '''Oswald:''' Still getting those house samples, hm?

'''Benjamin:''' Yea... it's such a pain in the arse. Why do I always have to do it?

'''Oswald:''' You wouldn't be complaining if you had read the contract, dude.

'''Benjamin:''' Whatever. I've already gotten the wood from the walls, I guess I'll go upstairs to get a sample there, looks like the stairs are very long, though...

'''Oswald:''' Just go ahead. We need everything we can.

'''Benjamin:''' Alright, then. This better not be a waste of time.

''''''
'''

''' '''Oswald:''' Hey, you there?

'''Benjamin:''' Yeah, just a couple more steps and... done! I can hammer off a piece of the wall, and I can finally return home- ...Where did the stairs go?

'''Oswald:''' Say that again?

'''Benjamin:''' The stairs are gone! And now that I realize it... This area is a hell of a lot more different than anything we have yet seen in [[Level 39]]! Oh god, don't tell me...

'''Oswald:''' Remain calm. Just describe your surroundings. I'll see if I can determine where you went.

'''Benjamin:''' Uhhh, okay... It looks like an attic; the walls are pink, the floor's a bluish-grey, and there's a window, but beyond it, there's a bright light.

'''Oswald:''' I see... could you send a picture?

'''Benjamin:''' Right. Hold on a sec... okay, you should be receiving it.

'''Oswald:''' ...Ben, you might not want to hear this, but I think you've discovered a new level.

'''Benjamin:''' Dear god...

'''Oswald:''' Just stay calm, okay? We'll send some people to [[Level 39]] to try to find you. Anything else you can describe?

'''Benjamin:''' Yeah, uhhh... the environment seems to be shifting. I saw a corridor in the distance that wasn't there before.

'''Oswald:''' ''Darn it, this was meant to be simple...''

'''Benjamin:''' Hmm?

'''Oswald:''' Nothing. Just try to find an exit. And use your [[Object 1|water]] sparingly.

'''Benjamin:''' Got it. Time to go-

''''''
'''

''' '''Oswald:''' Ben? Ben..! Do you hear me? Ben!

''''''
'''

''' '''Oswald:''' Crap, I think we lost him!

==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== *It is believed that '''the Attic''' can be entered by breaking through the roofs of most levels. However, this is unconfirmed. *The most common entrance is through levels that are mainly composed of living spaces (like houses, apartments, hotels, and others) and levels that are predominantly composed of wood; levels like this include [[Level 9]], [[Level 11]], [[Level 27]], [[Level 39]], [[Level 188]], and more. To enter '''The Attic''' from such levels, one must find either an old and run-down flight of stairs or a trapdoor that is out of place. From there, one must either reach the top of the stairs or go through the trapdoor to enter '''The Attic'''. Wanderers should be mindful that encountering the staircase or trapdoor is very rare, and, as such, entry is inconsistent. *Reaching the "top" of the Tower of Babel in [[Level 999]] has been theorized to send you here. ===Exits=== *After wandering for an undetermined amount of time, one might find a trapdoor, a staircase, or a ladder. Once one has either gone through the trapdoor, reached the bottom of the staircase, or gone down the ladder, they will end up in the level they were previously in before having entered '''The Attic'''. ----
